title: TC001-Navigate from home to About Company via site navigation
description: Verifies users can open the navigation menu from the home page and reach the About Company page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Try a direct navigation to the About page because the home page has no interactive elements to open the nav menu.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/about", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=About Company').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The navigation menu could not be opened from the home page and the About/Company page could not be verified because the site pages are blank and have no interactive elements.

Observations:
- The home page showed a blank page with 0 interactive elements after waiting.
- Navigating directly to /about also displayed a blank page with 0 interactive elements.
- The browser tab title shows 'شركة مياه الشرب والصرف الصحي -' but the page content area is empty.
- Waited 3 seconds on the home page with no change in UI.
- No navigation menu or links were available to interact with on either route.

title: TC002-Navigate from home to Branches via site navigation
description: Verifies users can open the navigation menu from the home page and reach the branches listing page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the accessibility icon (index 108) to see if it reveals navigation controls or any way to access the site's menu.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the accessibility widget's inner element (index 110) to expand the accessibility panel and look for any navigation or links to reach the branches page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Branches').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The navigation menu could not be opened from the home page because the main site content did not load; only an accessibility widget is visible.

Observations:
- The page is blank except for a floating accessibility icon in the top-right corner.
- Interactive elements detected are the accessibility widget (icon and its iframe) and no site navigation links or menu buttons.
- Clicking the accessibility widget did not reveal any site navigation or links to reach the branches page.
- The page URL is http://localhost:5173 and the tab title shows 'شركة مياه الشرب والصرف الصحي -'.
- No branches listing or navigation elements were found on the page.

title: TC003-Navigate from home to News Archive via site navigation
description: Verifies users can reach the news archive page from the home page navigation and see news summaries.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the accessibility icon (index 112) to see if it reveals navigation or links to the news archive.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Try to open the accessibility widget/menu again by clicking the accessibility icon to see if it reveals navigation or a link to the news archive.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=News Archive').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The home page does not provide a navigation menu or a link to the news archive, so the news archive cannot be reached from the home page.

Observations:
- The home page rendered only an accessibility widget in the top-right; no site navigation, header, or content is visible.
- After waiting (2s + 3s) the page still showed no navigation links or news items.
- Clicking the accessibility icon twice did not reveal any site navigation or a link to a news archive.
- Browser interactive elements list shows only the accessibility control (index 112) and its shadow content; no menu or news links were present.
- Verification steps remaining: link to news archive not found and no news summaries to verify.

title: TC006-Verify core pages render primary content when opened directly
description: Verifies key informational pages load their primary content when accessed via static routes.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the static route /about-company and verify the about/company content page is displayed.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/about-company", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /branches (http://localhost:5173/branches) and verify whether the branches listing page content is displayed; if the page is also blank, report the issue and finish.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/branches", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=About Company').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Branches').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The informational pages failed to load — each static route displayed a blank page instead of content.

Observations:
- The root page (http://localhost:5173) appeared blank with no visible content or links.
- The /about-company page showed a blank page with no page content.
- The /branches page showed a blank page with no page content.
- Browser page stats reported zero interactive items and the screenshot is empty.

title: TC012-Projects archive page loads and displays project summaries
description: Verifies the projects archive page renders its list of project summaries.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/projects-archive and wait for the projects archive page to load, then check for a list of project summaries.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/projects-archive", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the accessibility icon to see if it reveals or unblocks the SPA content, then wait for the page to update and check for project summaries.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Projects Archive').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

Observations:
